symbols.name: V
symbols.def: Observed-node vertex set of the acyclic directed mixed graph G.

symbols.name: G
symbols.def: Known acyclic directed mixed graph on V, with directed edges E_dir and bidirected edges E_bidir.

symbols.name: u
symbols.def: Parent endpoint of the target directed edge u -> v in G.

symbols.name: v
symbols.def: Child endpoint of the target directed edge u -> v in G.

symbols.name: P_v
symbols.def: Ordered parent tuple P_v = (p_1,...,p_q) = pa(v).

symbols.name: q
symbols.def: Number of parents q = |P_v|.

symbols.name: s_u
symbols.def: Unique index such that p_{s_u} = u in the ordered tuple P_v.

symbols.name: Lambda
symbols.def: Directed-edge coefficient matrix with Lambda_ab = 0 whenever a -> b is absent from G.

symbols.name: B_Lambda
symbols.def: Directed-path transfer matrix B_Lambda = (I - Lambda)^(-T).

symbols.name: epsilon
symbols.def: Exogenous error vector in the representation X = B_Lambda epsilon.

symbols.name: X
symbols.def: Observed node vector satisfying X = B_Lambda epsilon.

symbols.name: I_v
symbols.def: Ordered witness tuple I_v = (i_1,...,i_q) used in the TDE non-intersecting-path certificate for P_v.

symbols.name: Pi_v
symbols.def: Non-intersecting directed path system Pi_v = (pi_1,...,pi_q) pairing i_r to p_r.

symbols.name: m
symbols.def: Order of the observable joint cumulants used in the transfer construction.

symbols.name: C
symbols.def: Family C = (C_1,...,C_q) where each C_r is an ordered (m-2)-tuple of observed nodes used to augment row r of the transfer system.

symbols.name: m_prime
symbols.def: Alternative cumulant order used for a second admissible family.

symbols.name: C_prime
symbols.def: Alternative admissible family C_prime = (C_prime_1,...,C_prime_q) used to compare observable transfer designs.

symbols.name: Mcal_m(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v)
symbols.def: Set of order-m graph-uniform cumulant-admissible families C for the target edge u -> v relative to the witness (I_v,Pi_v).

symbols.name: m_star(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v)
symbols.def: Smallest m for which Mcal_m(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) is nonempty; infinity if no such m exists.

symbols.name: K_X(I_v,C)
symbols.def: Observable transfer matrix with entry [K_X(I_v,C)]_{r,s} = kappa_X(i_r,p_s,C_r).

symbols.name: k_X(v;I_v,C)
symbols.def: Observable transfer vector with entry [k_X(v;I_v,C)]_r = kappa_X(i_r,v,C_r).

symbols.name: lambda_Pv,v
symbols.def: Parent-effect vector lambda_Pv,v = (Lambda_{p_1 v},...,Lambda_{p_q v})^T.

symbols.name: lambda_uv
symbols.def: Target direct-effect coefficient lambda_uv = Lambda_{uv}.

symbols.name: Khat_n(I_v,C)
symbols.def: Empirical k-statistic analogue of K_X(I_v,C) computed from n i.i.d. observations of X.

symbols.name: khat_n(v;I_v,C)
symbols.def: Empirical k-statistic analogue of k_X(v;I_v,C) computed from n i.i.d. observations of X.

symbols.name: lambdahat_uv,n(C)
symbols.def: Plug-in estimator obtained by replacing the observable cumulants in the determinant ratio for lambda_uv by their empirical k-statistics.

assumptions.condition: G is an acyclic directed mixed graph on V and u -> v is a directed edge in G.
X = B_Lambda epsilon with B_Lambda = (I - Lambda)^(-T) and Lambda_ab = 0 whenever a -> b is absent from G.
The error vector epsilon satisfies the connected-set Markov property with respect to the bidirected part of G.

standard.name: connected-set Markov property for bidirected errors

assumptions.condition: There exist an ordered witness tuple I_v and a non-intersecting path system Pi_v pairing i_r to p_r for r = 1,...,q, so the TDE v-rank criterion certifies generic identifiability of lambda_uv.
The directed coefficients and the nonzero connected error cumulants lie outside the algebraic exceptional set on which the relevant witness minors vanish.
All joint moments of X up to order 2m are finite.

standard.name: finite high-order moment condition for cumulant CLTs

assumptions.condition: For the chosen admissible pair (m,C), |det K_X(I_v,C)| >= c0 for some constant c0 > 0.

definitions.name: Witness-row augmentation search handle
definitions.construction: Starting from the TDE witness (I_v,Pi_v), enumerate m = 2,3,... and families C = (C_1,...,C_q) in (V^{m-2})^q; retain a family when the multilinear cumulant expansion of each row map j -> kappa_X(i_r,j,C_r) under X = B_Lambda epsilon leaves exactly one nonvanishing connected-error contribution across j in P_v union {v}, so the same row scalar multiplies every column.

definitions.name: Order-m graph-uniform cumulant-admissible family
definitions.construction: { C = (C_1,...,C_q) in (V^{m-2})^q : outside a proper algebraic subset of parameter pairs (Lambda,epsilon) satisfying ass:linear-mixed-representation and ass:connected-set-markov on G, for each r in {1,...,q} there exists a nonzero scalar A_r(Lambda,epsilon) independent of s such that kappa_X(i_r,p_s,C_r) = A_r(Lambda,epsilon) (B_Lambda)_{p_s,i_r} for every s in {1,...,q} and kappa_X(i_r,v,C_r) = A_r(Lambda,epsilon) (B_Lambda)_{v,i_r} }

definitions.name: Minimal graph-uniform admissible cumulant order
definitions.construction: m_star(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) = min{ m >= 2 : Mcal_m(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) != emptyset }, with value infinity if the set is empty.

definitions.name: Sample cumulant plug-in estimator
definitions.construction: lambdahat_uv,n(C) = det(Khat_n(I_v,C) with column s_u replaced by khat_n(v;I_v,C)) / det Khat_n(I_v,C).

statements.statement: Under ass:acyclic-admg, ass:linear-mixed-representation, ass:connected-set-markov, ass:tde-witness, and ass:genericity, does the search handle in def:transfer-search-handle always terminate with some finite order m and some family C in Mcal_m(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v)?
statements.justification: The unresolved mathematical content is not Cramer's rule but whether every hidden TDE witness admits a finite observable cumulant transfer built from graph-legible row augmentations.
statements.gap: TramontanoDrtonEtesami2024 supplies the generic identifiability certificate and the hidden row system in Eq. (3.9), but not an observable-side construction; KivvaSalehkaleybarKiyavash2023 and TramontanoKivvaSalehkaleybarDrtonKiyavash2025 solve only special proxy or underspecified-IV families with hand-built moment equations.
statements.consumer: A positive answer would convert the TDE frontier into a finite symbolic search problem and make the closed-form estimator available exactly where the graphical criterion certifies point identification.

statements.statement: Fix m >= 2 and let C belong to Mcal_m(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v). Then, generically, K_X(I_v,C) lambda_Pv,v = k_X(v;I_v,C) and det K_X(I_v,C) != 0.
statements.justification: Once an admissible family is in hand, the hidden TDE row system becomes an observable linear system in cumulants.
statements.gap: TramontanoDrtonEtesami2024 identifies the relevant B_Lambda submatrix through the hidden rank system around Eq. (3.9), while FoygelDraismaDrton2012 and BarberDrtonSturmaWeihs2022 stay on the covariance layer and therefore do not treat connected-set cumulant cancellation under general non-factor confounding.
statements.consumer: The row system recovers the full parent-effect vector of v from one observable matrix, which is the algebraic bridge needed for direct-effect formulas and simultaneous parent-vector estimation.

statements.statement: Under the conditions of thm:cumulant-row-system, lambda_uv equals det(K_X(I_v,C) with column s_u replaced by k_X(v;I_v,C)) divided by det K_X(I_v,C).
statements.justification: This is the kernel claim: an explicit observable rational functional for the target direct effect.
statements.gap: KivvaSalehkaleybarKiyavash2023 gives a cross-moment ratio for a single-proxy graph, and TramontanoKivvaSalehkaleybarDrtonKiyavash2025 gives higher-order cumulant formulas for the proxy and underspecified-IV lvLiNGAM setups in Sections 3.1 and 3.2; neither paper treats arbitrary TDE-certified ADMG witnesses under general bidirected confounding.
statements.consumer: The ratio furnishes a direct estimand for plug-in inference and a constructive observable target on graphs where the TDE criterion identifies lambda_uv but no covariance or proxy-specialized formula applies.

statements.statement: If C belongs to Mcal_m(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) and C_prime belongs to Mcal_{m_prime}(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v), then the determinant ratios in thm:closed-form-coefficient coincide as rational functions on the generic model class.
statements.justification: The identifying functional should depend on the edge and witness, not on a numerically convenient augmentation choice.
statements.gap: Neither the hidden witness formalism in TramontanoDrtonEtesami2024 nor the special-case cumulant identities in TramontanoKivvaSalehkaleybarDrtonKiyavash2025 establish agreement across distinct observable admissible families.
statements.consumer: Witness invariance permits software to select a stable admissible family without changing the estimand that is reported or the target of inference.

statements.statement: Fix m >= 2 and C in Mcal_m(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v). Under ass:finite-moments and ass:denominator-margin, lambdahat_uv,n(C) converges in probability to lambda_uv and sqrt(n) (lambdahat_uv,n(C) - lambda_uv) converges in distribution to a mean-zero normal law whose variance is obtained by the multivariate delta method applied to the vector of sample cumulants entering Khat_n(I_v,C) and khat_n(v;I_v,C).
statements.justification: A constructive formula is materially stronger once it supports a standard pointwise inference theory.
statements.gap: TramontanoDrtonEtesami2024 explicitly leaves confidence intervals open, and MareisSturmaDrton2026 gives the analogous asymptotic normality result only for covariance half-trek estimators rather than higher-order cumulant ratios under general confounding.
statements.consumer: This proposition yields plug-in standard errors and Wald intervals after a concrete admissible cumulant design has been fixed.

statements.statement: If q = 1, P_v = (u), and the empty family C = (empty tuple) belongs to Mcal_2(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v), then thm:closed-form-coefficient reduces to lambda_uv = Cov(X_{i_1}, X_v) / Cov(X_{i_1}, X_u).
statements.justification: The general cumulant formula should collapse to the classical covariance IV ratio when a second-moment witness exists.
statements.gap: TramontanoDrtonEtesami2024 uses the covariance IV ratio as a motivating example, but does not embed it as the order-2 special case of a graph-uniform cumulant transfer theorem.
statements.consumer: This reduction is the referee-checkable sanity benchmark separating genuinely higher-order identification from the familiar IV case.

statements.statement: Suppose ass:acyclic-admg, ass:linear-mixed-representation, ass:connected-set-markov, ass:tde-witness, and ass:genericity hold, and in addition each witness row i_r (r=1,...,q) is a directed SOURCE in G with An(i_r)={i_r} and is bidirected-disconnected from every error node that is an ancestor of some node in P_v union {v}. Then the empty order-2 family C with C_r = empty tuple for all r belongs to Mcal_2(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v); concretely K_X(I_v,C) = diag(kappa_epsilon(i_1,i_1),...,kappa_epsilon(i_q,i_q)) H with H_{r,s}=(B_Lambda)_{p_s,i_r} and k_X(v;I_v,C)=diag(kappa_epsilon(i_r,i_r)) b with b_r=(B_Lambda)_{v,i_r}. Consequently, on the generic model class, det K_X(I_v,C) != 0 and lambda_Pv,v = K_X(I_v,C)^{-1} k_X(v;I_v,C), so by thm:closed-form-coefficient the entire parent-effect vector of v, in particular lambda_uv, is identified by the observable ORDER-2 (covariance) determinant ratio. Hence on the source-isolated witness subclass the observable identifying functional exists UNCONDITIONALLY at covariance order (m_star=2), and prop:iv-reduction is the q=1 special case.
statements.justification: Converts the conditional kernel into an UNCONDITIONAL observable-formula theorem on a named subclass of TDE witnesses: source-isolated witness rows admit the empty order-2 admissible family, so the covariance-order determinant ratio identifies the full parent-effect vector with no conditional-on-admissibility caveat.
statements.gap: TramontanoDrtonEtesami2024 gives only the hidden rank certificate; the paper's kernel results are conditional on an admissible family existing (which oeq:finite-order-transfer shows can fail). This theorem exhibits a concrete graph-legible subclass where the observable formula provably exists at covariance order.
statements.consumer: A checkable graph condition (source-isolated witness rows, bidirected-disconnected from relevant ancestors) under which the estimator is available from second moments alone, generalizing the classical IV covariance ratio to the full multi-parent effect vector.

statements.statement: Characterize m_star(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) from the bidirected connected-set pattern and the witness Pi_v, and determine whether for every m < m_star(G,u,v,I_v,Pi_v) there exist two generic laws in the model class that agree on all observable cumulants up to order m yet differ in lambda_uv.
statements.justification: Higher-order cumulants become statistically expensive quickly, so the least sufficient order is a first-class mathematical object.
statements.gap: TramontanoDrtonEtesami2024 justifies genericity through cumulants of arbitrary order, and SchkodaRobevaDrton2024 builds graph-dependent cumulant rank matrices, but neither paper gives a sharp graph-level lower frontier for direct-effect identification order on the TDE class.
statements.consumer: A sharp order frontier would tell applied users which cumulants are worth estimating and would turn lower-order failures into formal impossibility statements.

estimand_functional: lambda_uv = det(K_X(I_v,C) with column s_u replaced by k_X(v;I_v,C)) / det K_X(I_v,C)

sampling_model.design: i.i.d. observational sampling from the law of X in the linear non-Gaussian ADMG model
sampling_model.units: n independent draws X^(1),...,X^(n) of the full observed node vector X
sampling_model.treatment: not design-based; the target is the structural edge coefficient on u -> v
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: No nonparametric nuisance regression is estimated; the observable ingredients are the joint cumulants entering a chosen admissible pair (m,C), estimated by unbiased k-statistics.

bibliography.citation: Daniele Tramontano, Mathias Drton, and Jalal Etesami (2024). Parameter identification in linear non-Gaussian causal models under general confounding. arXiv:2405.20856.
Yaroslav Kivva, Saber Salehkaleybar, and Negar Kiyavash (2023). A Cross-Moment Approach for Causal Effect Estimation. arXiv:2306.06263.
Daniele Tramontano, Yaroslav Kivva, Saber Salehkaleybar, Mathias Drton, and Negar Kiyavash (2025). Causal Effect Identification in lvLiNGAM from Higher-Order Cumulants. ICML 2025; arXiv:2506.05202.
Daniela Schkoda, Elina Robeva, and Mathias Drton (2024). Causal Discovery of Linear Non-Gaussian Causal Models with Unobserved Confounding. arXiv:2408.04907.
Rina Foygel, Jan Draisma, and Mathias Drton (2012). Half-trek criterion for generic identifiability of linear structural equation models. Annals of Statistics 40(3):1682-1713.
Rina Foygel Barber, Mathias Drton, Nils Sturma, and Luca Weihs (2022). Half-Trek Criterion for Identifiability of Latent Variable Models. Annals of Statistics 50(6):3174-3196.
Leopold Mareis, Nils Sturma, and Mathias Drton (2026). Semiparametric Inference for Half-Trek Estimators in Linear Structural Equation Models. arXiv:2606.26931.

comparator_promise_table.comparator_claim: Theorem 3.8 and Eq. (3.9) give a necessary-and-sufficient graphical certificate and a hidden B_Lambda row system for generic direct-effect identifiability under general confounding.
A single-proxy linear SCM admits a causal-effect formula based on cross moments and Gaussian data cannot identify the effect.
Sections 3.1 and 3.2 identify causal effects from higher-order cumulants in single-proxy and underspecified-IV lvLiNGAM families.
Half-trek systems recover generic direct effects as rational functions of covariances in linear SEMs.
Latent-factor half-trek criteria recover direct effects as rational functions of the observed covariance matrix.
HTC-based rational estimators admit asymptotic normality with closed-form variance expressions.

tldr: The kernel is to replace the hidden TDE witness in B_Lambda by an observable cumulant-transfer system. For a TDE-certified edge u -> v, the proposal defines graph-uniform cumulant-admissible row augmentations C that force each row of a joint-cumulant matrix to factor into a common nuisance scalar times the relevant entry of B_Lambda. Conditional on such a family, the parent-effect row at v solves an observable linear system K_X(I_v,C) lambda_Pv,v = k_X(v;I_v,C), and the target coefficient lambda_uv is a Cramer-rule ratio of determinants built only from observable higher-order cumulants. The same construction nests the covariance IV ratio when an order-2 witness exists, and it supports a plug-in k-statistic estimator with delta-method inference away from weak denominators. The genuinely open content is whether every TDE witness admits a finite admissible family and what graph feature determines the least required cumulant order.

project_justification.gap: The literature has a complete generic graphical certificate for direct-effect identification under arbitrary bidirected confounding, but it still lacks an observable identifying functional in the general class where the TDE proof stops at a hidden B_Lambda row system and proxy- or IV-specific cumulant formulas do not apply.
project_justification.niche: The open niche is constructive identification exactly on the TDE frontier: not a new graph criterion, and not another special proxy or IV formula, but a graph-to-cumulant transfer principle that turns a hidden-rank certificate into an observable determinant ratio whenever a finite admissible augmentation exists.
project_justification.fill: The proposal fills that niche by formulating graph-uniform cumulant-admissible witnesses and a concrete transfer-search handle, proving the observable row system and direct-effect determinant ratio conditional on such a witness, and isolating finite-order existence and sharp order selection as first-class open questions.

related_work: Tramontano, Drton, and Etesami (2024) is the anchor because Theorem 3.8 gives the complete generic direct-effect criterion under general confounding and Eq. (3.9) exposes the hidden B_Lambda row system, but the paper estimates through residual-independence optimization rather than an observable formula. Kivva, Salehkaleybar, and Kiyavash (2023) provide the prototype single-proxy cross-moment ratio and the corresponding Gaussian impossibility result, so they are the cleanest low-dimensional analogue of a cumulant ratio. Tramontano, Kivva, Salehkaleybar, Drton, and Kiyavash (2025) show in Sections 3.1 and 3.2 that higher-order cumulant equations can identify effects in single-proxy and underspecified-IV lvLiNGAM families, but those formulas are tailored to those subclasses rather than arbitrary ADMG witnesses with general bidirected confounding. Foygel, Draisma, and Drton (2012) and Barber, Drton, Sturma, and Weihs (2022) provide the determinant-ratio and row-solving template on the covariance layer, which motivates the matrix shape here but not the higher-order connected-set cancellations. Schkoda, Robeva, and Drton (2024) reinforce that graph-dependent cumulant matrices are the right observable objects for latent-confounded LiNGAM, yet their aim is discovery and finite compatible effect sets rather than a closed identifying functional for a TDE-certified edge. Mareis, Sturma, and Drton (2026) shows how semiparametric inference can be layered onto a rational SEM formula once the constructive estimand exists, which is the inferential template adapted here after the cumulant-transfer step.
honest_scope: The settled claims are conditional: once an admissible cumulant family is available, the observable row system, determinant ratio, IV reduction, and pointwise delta-method inference follow. The standing open content is the finite-order existence question oeq:finite-order-transfer and the sharp graph-level order frontier in oeq:minimal-order-sharpness. Weak-denominator uniform coverage remains outside prop:plugin-delta-method.
